frameworks.name: Bayesian inference of environmental priors
frameworks.one_line: observer gradually refines an estimate of the environmental prior from experience

frameworks.name: neural attractor dynamics
frameworks.one_line: persistent activity attractors aligned to common stimulus values via learned synaptic connectivity

frameworks.name: experience-dependent learning model
frameworks.one_line: biases emerge from history-shaped circuit modulation rather than fixed parameters

frameworks.name: fixed-bias model
frameworks.one_line: alternative in which response biases do not adapt to stimulus history

core_question: Do systematic biases in working memory reflect experience-dependent learning of environmental priors implemented in neural circuit connectivity?
key_assumptions: observers infer environmental priors gradually through experience
synaptic connectivity in a working memory circuit is modulated by stimulus history
persistent neural activity encodes the stimulus estimate as attractors
manipulations: heterogeneous stimulus (color) distributions that did not match reported population biases
model class: experience-dependent learning vs fixed-bias
key_findings: humans show systematic biases toward common feature values when retaining color over short delays
most subjects' response distributions are better fit by experience-dependent learning models than by fixed-bias models
biases can be reproduced when synaptic connectivity is shaped by experienced stimulus history
resulting neural activity attractors align to commonly experienced stimulus values
interpretations: systematic limitations in working memory reflect efficient representations of inferred environmental structure
history-dependent synaptic modulation provides a circuit basis for prior-driven memory biases
